context.business_name: Scott Mission | You & Me Canada Gifts

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 6.1 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a flat.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 2, 1, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark navy/charcoal board-and-batten siding on upper floors, cream/white painted ground floor
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: upper floors appear to have vertical board-and-batten or panel cladding in dark navy/charcoal; ground floor is painted white/cream with institutional signage
photo_observations.window_details: second floor has one large 6-pane grid window (illuminated), ground floor has at least 2 windows with multi-pane glazing visible from interior light; upper floor windows difficult to discern in darkness
photo_observations.door_details: institutional entry door, appears to be glass or glazed panel, centered on ground floor
photo_observations.cornice_details: light-coloured horizontal band between ground and upper floors, acts as a simple cornice/string course separating the white base from the dark upper cladding
photo_observations.decorative_details: minimal decoration; institutional character with clean lines
photo_observations.storefront_description: institutional ground floor with Scott Mission signage; glass entry door and illuminated windows
photo_observations.signage_observed: Scott Mission logo and name (blue/yellow cross emblem) on white background at ground level
photo_observations.alterations_visible: building appears to have been re-clad; upper floors covered in dark board-and-batten or panel siding over original brick; ground floor painted/re-finished for institutional use
photo_observations.notes: Nighttime photo with limited visibility. Building is an institutional/charitable use (Scott Mission). Ground floor cream/white with mission signage, upper floors dark navy/charcoal cladding. Adjacent brick building visible to right. Photo taken at 12:56am, some motion blur but key features discernible.

facade_description: 346 Spadina Ave presents a street-facing facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 6.1 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a flat. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a dark navy board-and-batten upper floors with cream/white painted ground floor palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 2, 1, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
